substep.title: Pre-Mortem Pass
substep.action: Before cataloguing, run a pre-mortem over the scan findings: assume this approach has failed in production six months from now — what were the causes? This surfaces failure modes the category scans may have under-weighted, especially at the seams between categories (a performance fix that opens a security hole; an operational shortcut that risks data integrity).

substep.title: Grade Each Risk
substep.action:
For each risk identified, record: - Category: which lens it falls under - Risk: what could go wrong, stated concretely - Evidence: file:line, URL, or thoughts/ alias grounding the risk - Likelihood (High / Medium / Low): the evidence-grounded probability it materializes — justify with the evidence, not a hunch ("HIGH: input is user-controlled and reaches the query unescaped [cite]") - Impact (High / Medium / Low): severity if it does materialize - Mitigation: the standard countermeasure for this risk class, stated factually ("mitigated by parameterized queries") — NOT a remediation order for our code. Where our codebase already applies the mitigation elsewhere, cite it.
Rank by likelihood × impact; High/High risks lead the register.

substep.title: Compose the Register
substep.action:
Compose the body per references/research/document-format body-skeleton. Fixed top: the approach assessed, and a Summary ranking the top risks (the High/High and High/Medium first). Variable middle: the risk register grouped by category, entries ordered by severity within each. Bottom: Open Questions (categories assessed from internal evidence only, scans deferred at the web gate).
Adversarial honesty: if the approach is genuinely low-risk, say so — but surface the residual and low risks rather than declaring all-clear. A scan that found nothing worth noting is a scan that did not look hard enough.

check.template-output:
Present the register for review before persistence.
[a] Revise (re-scan a category, sharpen a likelihood grade) [c] Continue to persistence [y] YOLO
